content:
AUDIENCE:
This reader is an 8-year-old child who is curious about the world and enjoys imaginative stories. They have a basic understanding of concepts like life and death but might find complex philosophical ideas confusing. They care about fun, adventure, and simple explanations. They would likely be bored by long, complicated sentences or adult themes. A playful and lighthearted tone with vivid images and a bit of humor would engage them best.

===BEGIN===
I did it! I found the secret to living forever! No more bedtime or getting tired. I could play video games all day, eat ice cream whenever I wanted, and never have to go to school again.

But just when I was celebrating, a shadow appeared. It was Death! But instead of being scary, Death smiled and handed me a shiny business card. “When you realize living forever is actually super boring,” Death said, “call this number. I have a special job just for you.”

I looked at the card. It had a picture of a friendly skeleton wearing a suit and holding a giant clock. Maybe living forever isn’t as fun as I thought. Maybe it’s okay to have an ending, so new stories can begin.

I put the card in my pocket and smiled. Life is an adventure — and sometimes, even Death has a fun surprise!
===END===
